chapter: JEF P104 – A Question of Trust
title: A Question of Trust – Easy SAQs

questions.question: Who is the author of the lesson ‘A Question of Trust’?
questions.answer: The author of the lesson is Victor Canning.

questions.question: What kind of person was Horace Danby in society?
questions.answer: Horace Danby was considered a respectable and honest citizen.

questions.question: What was Horace Danby’s profession?
questions.answer: Horace Danby was a locksmith by profession.

questions.question: What did Horace Danby like to collect?
questions.answer: He liked to collect rare and expensive books.

questions.question: Why did Horace steal every year?
questions.answer: He stole every year to get money to buy rare books.

questions.question: Where did Horace plan to commit the robbery?
questions.answer: Horace planned the robbery at Shotover Grange.

questions.question: What illness did Horace Danby suffer from?
questions.answer: Horace Danby suffered from hay fever.

questions.question: Who was Sherry?
questions.answer: Sherry was the dog in the house at Shotover Grange.

questions.question: Why did Horace feel confident before the robbery?
questions.answer: He felt confident because he had planned carefully and the servants were away.

questions.question: Whom did Horace meet inside the house?
questions.answer: Horace met a young lady inside the house.

questions.question: What did the young lady pretend to be?
questions.answer: She pretended to be the owner’s wife.

questions.question: What request did the young lady make to Horace?
questions.answer: She asked Horace to open the safe for her.

questions.question: What was kept inside the safe?
questions.answer: Valuable jewels were kept inside the safe.

questions.question: Why did Horace remove his gloves?
questions.answer: He removed his gloves to light the young lady’s cigarette.

questions.question: What mistake did Horace make during the robbery?
questions.answer: He left his fingerprints on the safe.

questions.question: Why was Horace arrested by the police?
questions.answer: He was arrested because his fingerprints were found on the safe.

questions.question: What did Horace become in prison?
questions.answer: Horace became an assistant librarian in prison.

questions.question: What quality best describes the young lady?
questions.answer: She was clever and deceptive.

questions.question: What is the main theme of the story?
questions.answer: The main theme of the story is trust and deception.

questions.question: Why is the title ‘A Question of Trust’ suitable?
questions.answer: The title is suitable because misplaced trust leads to Horace’s downfall.

questions.question: What idea is proved false in the story?
questions.answer: The idea of honour among thieves is proved false.

questions.question: What kind of morality did Horace follow?
questions.answer: Horace followed selective morality.

questions.question: Why did Horace help the young lady?
questions.answer: He helped her to avoid being reported to the police.

questions.question: What lesson does the story teach?
questions.answer: The story teaches that crime ultimately leads to punishment.

questions.question: What human weakness caused Horace’s failure?
questions.answer: Misplaced trust caused Horace’s failure.
